prompt: A monumental surreal stone landscape with three parallel aqueduct terraces filling the entire frame. In each separate terrace, water is clearly rushing steeply downward in bright realistic cascades. Yet the terraces are arranged in a single continuous composition so that together they become one giant river that appears unmistakably to climb upward into the sky. The contradiction must feel inevitable, not chaotic. Forced perspective, impossible but believable architecture, cinematic daylight, pale stone, luminous mist, high realism, no people, no vehicles, no signs, no text, no numbers, no symbols, no charts, no diagrams, no decorative split panels.
visual_logic: Three separate aqueduct channels visibly send water downward, yet together they align into one continuous river rising into the sky, making the local direction and the overall direction contradict each other at once.
